name: Fried Apple Pies
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp Lard or Solid Vegetable Shortening
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the fryer.
2. In a saucepan, melt the butter.
3. Add the apples and sauté for 2 minutes.
4. Stir in the cinnamon, nutmeg, 1/4 cup sugar, and brown sugar. Continue sautéing for 1 minute, stirring constantly to dissolve the sugar.
5. Add the water and lemon juice. Bring the liquid to a boil.
6. Add cornstarch dissolved in 1 tbsp water. Whisk the vanilla and slurry into the liquid.
7. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the apples are soft. Remove from the heat and cool completely.
8. In a mixing bowl, sift the flour, sugar, salt, and baking powder together. Cut in the lard until it resembles coarse meal.
9. In a small bowl, whisk the egg and milk together. Gradually add the egg mixture to the flour mixture, working it to form a thick dough.
10. Break the dough into 12 equal portions. On a lightly floured surface, roll the dough pieces into thin rounds, about 5 inches in diameter.
11. Place 1/4 cup of the filling in the center of each round, fold over and crimp the edges with a fork.
12. Fry the pies, two to three at a time, in the hot oil until golden brown. Remove from the oil and drain on a paper-lined plate.
13. Powder the pies with powdered sugar.
14. Lay the pies on a platter and serve with vanilla bean ice cream. Garnish with drizzle of caramel sauce, powdered sugar, and fresh mint.
